data.narrative:
Stoke Space is a Series D-stage space and aerospace company founded by Andy Lapsa and Tom Feldman, currently valued at $1.0B post-money following $860M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers at the same or adjacent stage (including Astranis at $3.5B Series D, ICEYE at $2.8B Series E, and Varda Space Industries at $1.5B Series C) and 5 public-market benchmarks in the sector (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a standard Series D-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.5x to the $1.0B current valuation, yielding $1.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector comparables produces a median valuation of $1.3B, with the peer range spanning $1.0B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (36.9x median across the five public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, typical for private companies.

The two applicable methods converge on a triangulated range of $1.1B to $1.7B, with a median of $1.4B. This represents a 10–70% upside from the current $1.0B valuation. The primary uncertainty is revenue trajectory and customer concentration, which would refine any public-comparable analysis if disclosed.
